# experiments.flicker_vs_true_boundaries { #tslai.experiments.flicker_vs_true_boundaries }

```python
tslai.experiments.flicker_vs_true_boundaries(
    tracks,
    truth_states,
    sample,
    state=0,
    eps=0.0,
)
```

Split a sample's segment-boundary flicker into at-true vs. off-true boundaries.

The §7.3 decision input: blocked EM is adequate (``bp/`` unnecessary) when flicker
at non-true boundaries is small relative to the discontinuity at true boundaries.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**tracks**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per-sample painting (sample id -> list of segments with a ``.posterior``).

<code>[**truth_states**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per-sample census-truth segments (sample id -> list of ``(left, right, state)``).

<code>[**sample**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sample id to score.

<code>[**state**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Ancestry state whose posterior discontinuity is measured (default 0).

<code>[**eps**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Tolerance (bp) for matching a segment boundary to a true switch.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``mean_flicker_off_true``, ``mean_flicker_at_true`` (mean ``\|ΔP(state)\|`` at each kind of boundary) and the counts ``n_off_true``, ``n_at_true``.
